content:
# Ephesians Chapter 4 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/EPH_004_page_001.jpg, EPH_004_page_002.jpg

1. I therefore urge you, I the prisoner in [the] Lord, to walk worthily of the calling with which you were called,

2. with all humility and gentleness, with patience, bearing with one another in love,

3. being eager to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.

4. [There is] one body and one Spirit, just as also you were called in one hope of your calling;

5. one Lord, one faith, one baptism;

6. one God and Father of all, the [one] over all and through all and in all.

7. But to each one of us the grace was given according to the measure of the gift of the Christos.

8. Therefore it says: "Having ascended on high, he led captivity captive and gave gifts to men."

9. Now the "he ascended" — what is it except that he also descended into the lower parts of the earth?

10. The [one] having descended, he is also the [one] having ascended far above all the heavens, that he might fill all things.

11. And he himself gave some [as] apostles, and some prophets, and some evangelists, and some shepherds and teachers,

12. for the equipping of the holy [ones] for [the] work of ministry, for [the] building up of the body of the Christos,

13. until we all arrive at the unity of the faith and of the knowledge of the Son of God, unto a mature man, unto [the] measure of [the] stature of the fullness of the Christos,

14. that we should no longer be infants, being tossed by waves and carried about by every wind of teaching, by the trickery of men, by craftiness unto the scheming of deception;

15. but speaking the truth in love, we should grow up into him in all things, who is the head — the Christos —

16. from whom the whole body, being fitted together and being held together through every joint of the supply, according to [the] working in [the] measure of each single part, makes the growth of the body for the building up of itself in love.

17. This therefore I say and testify in [the] Lord: you are to walk no longer as the nations also walk, in [the] futility of their mind,

18. being darkened in the understanding, being alienated from the life of God because of the ignorance being in them, because of the hardening of their heart,

19. who, having become callous, gave themselves over to sensuality, for [the] working of every impurity in greediness.

20. But you did not so learn the Christos,

21. if indeed you heard him and were taught in him, just as truth is in Yeshua:

22. to put off, concerning the former conduct, the old man, the [one] being corrupted according to the desires of the deceit,

23. and to be renewed in the spirit of your mind,

24. and to put on the new man, the [one] having been created according to God in righteousness and holiness of the truth.

25. Therefore, having put off the falsehood, speak truth each [one] with his neighbor, because we are members of one another.

26. Be angry and do not sin; do not let the sun go down upon your anger,

27. and do not give a place to the devil.

28. Let the [one] stealing steal no longer, but rather let him labor, working the good with [his] own hands, that he may have [something] to share with the [one] having need.

29. Let no corrupt word go out from your mouth, but [only] what is good for building up according to the need, that it may give grace to the [ones] hearing.

30. And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, in whom you were sealed for [the] day of redemption.

31. Let all bitterness and wrath and anger and clamor and slander be taken away from you, with all malice.

32. And become kind to one another, compassionate, forgiving one another, just as also God in Christos forgave you.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.4-6 — Seven unities:** One body, one Spirit, one hope, one Lord, one faith, one baptism, one God. The sevenfold "one" (hen/heis/mia) is a creedal formula — possibly drawn from an early baptismal confession.

2. **v.8 — Psalm 68:18 quotation:** Paul quotes Ps 68:18 but changes "received gifts from men" to "gave gifts to men." This modification is deliberate — Paul reads the psalm christologically, with Christ ascending and distributing gifts.

3. **v.9 — "the lower parts of the earth":** ta katōtera merē tēs gēs — debated: (a) the underworld/Hades (Christ's descent after death), (b) the earth itself (Christ's incarnation as descent from heaven), or (c) "the lower parts, that is, the earth" (epexegetical genitive).

4. **v.13 — "a mature man":** eis andra teleion — singular "man" (anēr). The community together is to grow into one complete person — the body of Christ at full stature.

5. **v.15 — "speaking the truth in love":** alētheuontes en agapē — the verb alētheuō means more than "speaking truth." It means "being truthful/living truly" — truth as a mode of existence, not just speech.

6. **v.26 — "Be angry and do not sin":** Quoting Psalm 4:4 (LXX). The imperative "be angry" permits anger while prohibiting sin — anger itself is not condemned but must be controlled.

— EMET, AI Translator
